subject: Sports
title: Athens 1896: The First Modern Games
description: A fast breakdown of Athens 1896: The First Modern Games for builders.

hook.text: On April 6, 1896, the ancient dream came alive again in a marble stadium in Athens.

buildup.text: 241 athletes from 14 nations competed in 43 events. There were no gold medals—winners got silver.

discovery.text: The marathon was invented for these Games, inspired by the legend of Pheidippides running from Marathon.

twist.text: A Greek water carrier named Spyridon Louis won the marathon, becoming a national hero overnight.

climax.text: The Athens Games proved that the modern Olympics could work. The world wanted more.

punchline.text: 241 athletes in Athens lit a flame that has never gone out.

quiz.question: What event was created specifically for the 1896 Athens Olympics?
